key_insight: HAMNO combines hierarchical local-and-global neural operator layers with adaptive gating, and its physics-informed variant improves long-time stability and physical consistency for nonlinear dynamical PDEs.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a surrogate modeling route for FEM-style PDE problems where accurate rollout, limited data, and robustness to changing initial conditions are critical.
method: Local convolution + global spectral operator fusion for multi-scale spatial learning
Data-dependent gating to adaptively weight local vs. global information at each location
Physics-informed extension with strong-form residual loss and weak-form FEM test-function constraints
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on non-periodic Allen-Cahn, Cahn-Hilliard, and Swift-Hohenberg systems, showing better long-horizon prediction, data efficiency, OOD initial-condition robustness, and seed stability versus neural-operator baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on correct implementation of the multi-objective physics loss and centroid-based tetrahedral quadrature, which may add numerical and training complexity.
paper_title: HAMNO: A Hierarchical Adaptive Multi-scale Neural Operator with Physics-Informed Learning for Dynamical Systems
